100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 6, 1926, the United States faced growing tensions with Mexico over oil concessions, as American companies sought to protect their investments amid nationalization efforts. In Europe, reports emerged of escalating political unrest in Germany, where the Weimar Republic struggled to maintain stability against rising extremist movements. Meanwhile, in the realm of science, a significant advancement in radio technology was announced, promising to enhance communication across the globe.
